title: 💻 Claude Code Best Practices
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

1. **Context Management is Paramount**: The most successful Claude Code users obsessively manage context through CLAUDE.md files, aggressive /clear usage, documentation systems (dev docs, living plans), and token-efficient tool design. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices (Anthropic), Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, Getting\_Good\_Results, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use. **Sources**: 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, Getting\_Good\_Results, Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices. **Sources**: Getting\_Good\_Results, How\_Anthropic\_teams\_use, Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, Getting\_Good\_Results, Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, Building\_AI\_Factory. **Sources**: 6\_Weeks\_of\_Claude\_Code, what\_makes\_CC\_good, How\_I\_Use\_Every\_Feature. ❌ Don't: @-file docs (embeds entire file on every run) ✅ Do: "For complex usage or FooBarError, see path/to/docs.md" ❌ Don't: "Never use --foo-bar flag" (agent gets stuck) ✅ Do: "Never use --foo-bar; prefer --baz instead" ❌ Don't: Write comprehensive manual ✅ Do: Document what Claude gets wrong. **Sources**: if\_youre\_not\_using\_these, How\_I\_Use\_Every\_Feature, Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, if\_youre\_not\_using\_these, Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, Design\_Partner. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, How\_Anthropic\_teams\_use. **Sources**: 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, How\_I\_Use\_Every\_Feature, Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, Building\_AI\_Factory, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, How\_I\_Use\_Every\_Feature. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, if\_youre\_not\_using\_these. **Sources**: what\_makes\_CC\_good, Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, How\_Anthropic\_teams\_use, Poster\_Maker\_example. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, Building\_AI\_Factory, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use. **Sources**: Ultimate\_AI\_Coding\_Guide, 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, Getting\_Good\_Results. **Sources**: Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices, Building\_AI\_Factory. **Sources**: 6\_months\_hardcore\_use, Claude\_Code\_Best\_Practices.

title: 💻 Best Practices for Maximizing Claude Code Performance
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

Anthropic’s Claude Code is more than just a code completion tool; it’s an “agentic” development partner with direct access to your shell and file system, capable of performing tasks autonomously. When a session starts, Claude Code automatically checks for a `CLAUDE.md` file in your project's root directory. # Project Guidelines for Claude Code## 1. # Project Guidelines for Claude Code## 1. You can encapsulate complex, repetitive workflows into powerful commands shared across your team by creating markdown files in the `.claude/commands` directory. 2. **Claude:** (Generates the test code.). * Set up a hook that runs Claude in headless mode on every commit attempt to automatically add documentation to any new functions in the staged files. * `claude code --headless "Add JSDoc comments to all new functions in the staged files."`. The keys to success are providing clear project knowledge (`CLAUDE.md`), automating repetitive tasks (slash commands), and approaching complex problems with proven workflows (Explore→Plan). I mastered the Claude Code workflow. ## I mastered the Claude Code workflow.

Claude Code 技术干货

Write and run tests to verify the fix. ### ​ Create custom subagents. ### ​ Ask codebase questions. ### ​ Let Claude interview you. ## ​ Manage your session. ### ​ Use subagents for investigation. `use a subagent to review this code for edge cases`. ### ​ Resume conversations. ### ​ Run non-interactive mode. claude -p "List all API endpoints" --output-format json. claude -p "Analyze this log file" --output-format stream-json`. ### ​ Run multiple Claude sessions. ### ​ Fan out across files.

title: 💻 Claude Code: shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

[](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice#-hot). | [**Git Worktrees**](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows) | built-in | [![Image 42: Best Practice](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/best-practice.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2025007393290272904) Isolated git branches for parallel development — each agent gets its own working copy |. [](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice#-hot-1). *   after a mediocre fix — "knowing everything you know now, scrap this and implement the elegant solution" [![Image 55: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2017742752566632544). *   say "use subagents" to throw more compute at a problem — offload tasks to keep your main context clean and focused 👶 [![Image 57: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2017742755737555434). *   always start with [plan mode](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows)[![Image 58: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179845336527000). *   write detailed specs and reduce ambiguity before handing work off — the more specific you are, the better the output [![Image 61: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2017742752566632544). [![Image 62: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179840848597422). *   use [commands](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands) for your workflows instead of [sub-agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents)[![Image 63: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179847949500714). [![Image 64: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179850139000872). *   use ASCII diagrams a lot to understand your architecture [![Image 66: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2017742759218794768). *   [/sandbox](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing) to reduce permission prompts with file and network isolation [![Image 69: Boris](https://github.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/raw/main/!/tags/boris-cherny.svg)](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2021700506465579443). title: 💻 Claude Code /simplify and /batch Commands Guide
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code /simplify and /batch: Bundled Commands That Ship Code Faster. Claude Code /simplify runs 3-agent code review on your changes. Boris Cherny, the Claude Code PM, put it well: these bundled commands "automate much of the work it used to take to shepherd a pull request to production." `/simplify` is the automated code review step between "it works" and "it's ready to merge.". Claude Code's `/batch` command orchestrates large-scale changes across your codebase in parallel. Each `/batch` worker automatically runs `/simplify` on its own changes before committing. `/simplify` and `/batch` are bundled Claude Code commands -- they ship with every installation and work out of the box. `/simplify` and `/batch` shipped in Claude Code v2.1.63. `/simplify` and `/batch` signal where Claude Code is headed: bundled multi-agent workflows that handle common engineering patterns out of the box. If you want a development framework with pre-built agent orchestration, custom slash commands, and multi-agent patterns that work alongside these bundled commands, the ClaudeFast Code Kit ships with 18 specialized agents and a pipeline designed for exactly this kind of workflow.
excerpt: # Claude Code /simplify and /batch: Bundled Commands That Ship Code Faster. Claude Code /simplify runs 3-agent code review on your changes. Boris Cher...

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide - SmartScope
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

#!/bin/bash# parallel_processing.sh # Implement parallel processing()  { local  file = " $1 "  local  output_dir = " $2 "  echo  "Processing: $file "    "Optimize this code for performance"   " $file "   " $output_dir/$( " $file ") "} # Execute in parallel export      "*.py"  |    4   {}  echo  "✅ Parallel processing completed". #!/bin/bash# robust_processing.sh set    # Stop on error # Log configuration LOG_FILE ="claude_batch.log" exec  1  (  " $LOG_FILE ") exec  2  (  " $LOG_FILE "  & 2) # Error handling function()  { local  line_number = " $1 "  echo  "❌ Error occurred at line: $line_number "  echo  "📝 Check log file: $LOG_FILE "  exit  1} trap  'handle_error $LINENO'  # Process execution()  { local  max_retries = 3  local  retry_count = 0  while  ;  do  if    "Process these files"   300     ;  then  echo  "✅ Processing completed successfully"  return  0  else  (())  echo  "⚠️ Attempt $retry_count failed, retrying..."   5  fi  done  echo  "❌ Processing failed after $max_retries  attempts"  return  1} # Main processing()  { echo  "🚀 Starting batch processing..."  # Pre-processing  if  ;  then  echo  "❌ Input file not found"  exit  1  fi  # Execute processing   # Post-processing  if  ;  then  echo  "✅ Batch processing completed successfully"  else  echo  "❌ Output file is empty or missing"  exit  1  fi}  " $@ ".
excerpt: #!/bin/bash# parallel_processing.sh # Implement parallel processing()  { local  file = " $1 "  local  output_dir = " $2 "  echo  "Processing: $file " ...

title: 💻 Claude Code Voice Mode and /batch: Coding Workflow
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code Voice Mode and /batch: Coding Workflow. Anthropic began rolling out voice mode for Claude Code on March 3, 2026, giving approximately 5% of users the ability to control their terminal coding assistant entirely through spoken commands. This guide covers the technical implementation, practical workflows, hardware requirements, and limitations of Claude Code voice mode. For developers who spend hours daily in the terminal, voice mode represents a fundamental shift in how they can interact with AI coding tools, moving from typed prompts to natural language conversation while keeping hands free for other tasks. ## What Is Claude Code Voice Mode. Claude Code voice mode is a speech-to-text input layer for Anthropic's terminal-based coding assistant. The key distinction from browser-based voice interfaces is that Claude Code voice mode operates entirely within the terminal environment. The `/batch` command is a new feature released alongside voice mode that enables parallel execution of independent coding tasks.
